name: Sauteed Shrimp with Ancho Chiles and Garlic
recipeIngredient: 25 garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced
1 3/4 pounds rock or medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
3 large ancho chiles, wiped clean, stemmed, seeded, finely chopped
1 cup fish stock or clam juice
1 bunch Italian parsley, leaves only, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat.
2. Cook the garlic slices until tender but not brown. Transfer them with a slotted spoon to paper towels and reserve.
3. Turn the heat under the pan to high. Quickly toss the shrimp with the salt and pepper in a bowl.
4. When the oil is nearly smoking, add the shrimp. Saute, stirring and shaking the pan to prevent sticking, 3 to 4 minutes or just until the shrimp are still slightly undercooked. Remove from the heat.
5. With a slotted spoon, transfer the shrimp to a platter, leaving as much liquid in the pan as possible.
6. Return the pan to the burner and reduce the heat to medium.
7. Add the garlic slices and ancho chiles, sauteing, stirring frequently, until the oil begins to turn orange from the chiles.
8. Stir in the fish stock or clam juice, along with any collected juice on the platter. Add the lime juice and parsley, bring to a boil and remove from the heat.
9. Serve immediately over white rice.
